title: sarcandra glabra subsp. brachystachys
intro: Sarcandra glabra subsp. brachystachys, also known as the East Indian sarsaparilla, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Hernandiaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving rheumatism, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Sarcandra glabra subsp. brachystachys is typically prepared as a decoction or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
